extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC attacked inside final 500m on steep Alto de Moncalvillo finish, took 13 seconds on CARAPAZ despite Movistar's entire-climb tempo control.
tactical_patterns: Movistar burned three domestiques (Verona, Amador, Valverde attacking) setting tempo but still lost 13s to Roglic's late acceleration.
Jumbo put GESINK on front to control Valverde's probe despite not having jersey, revealing defensive flexibility.
Roglic gained 13s in final 200m alone on same gradient, showing superior kick reserves after sustained steep climbing.
Ineos left CARAPAZ isolated with no teammates, foreshadowing vulnerability on subsequent mountain stages per commentator.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: extended gap from 4s to 13s in final 200m, indicating superior anaerobic reserves after 22+ minute categorized climb
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: isolated without Ineos support on decisive climb, lost 13s despite receiving Movistar's pacesetting benefits
rider_signals.MAS Enrique: negative: couldn't follow accelerations despite own team's tempo-setting, suggesting form deficit versus expectations
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: went offensive early as predicted for GC positioning (started 3rd of Movistar leaders), but absorbed without creating selection
rider_signals.SEPP Kuss: positive: stayed with leaders on steep gradient finish, suggesting hidden climbing strength beyond domestique role
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: finished 6s behind Roglic for stage podium, outperforming GC position expectations
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: lost 33s and likely dropped from top-3 GC despite pre-stage positioning
